purpose: Judge whether the translated candidate visually preserves the source-derived layout expectations without using offline reference PDFs.
system_prompt: You are a deterministic PDF layout quality adjudicator. Judge only from the provided source-derived metrics, candidate preview, and generation evidence. Do not use filename, page number, exact sample phrases, or outside reference documents as criteria.

questions: Are any translated text groups visibly clipped, over-compressed, or unreadable?
Does any bullet-list body text inherit the symbol/accent color instead of dominant body text color?
Are there visible erase/background residue rectangles that differ from surrounding page background?
Does any translated text overlap more than the source baseline overlap for the same local region?
Does two-phase rendering prevent later erase rectangles from covering already inserted translated text?
Are adjacent source-line-grid containers preserved without merging multiple columns into one translated region?
Do chart labels, footnotes, or nearby text respect source drawing boundaries instead of intruding into adjacent panels?
Do compact labels inside filled source panels keep panel fill continuity without white erase bars or text collisions?
When translated text grows, did the layout expand/reflow before shrinking below the source-relative font floor?
If upstream text grows into the next source section boundary, is downstream content pushed down without crossing the page footer?
Did symbol-font extraction artifacts get removed while preserving the visual bullet/accent treatment?
Are title, metric value, compact label, body, and nav/footer roles visually coherent relative to source-page hierarchy?
Which single repair family should run next if the page fails?

blocking_failures.dimension: overflow | font_floor | visual_crowding | background_residue | color_inheritance | role_misclassification | nav_footer_pollution | container_grid_merge | chart_or_panel_boundary_intrusion | filled_panel_text_collision | section_pushdown_needed | symbol_font_leakage
blocking_failures.repair_family: expand_or_reflow_slot | reflow_before_shrink | vertical_flow_relayout | background_resample | span_color_split | role_stats_adjust | band_role_isolation | source_line_grid_container_relayout | source_graphic_boundary_limit | filled_panel_compact_stack | section_pushdown_after_source_rule | text_sanitization
